back_translation: He said to them, 'I tell you the truth, among those who are standing here, there are some who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God come with mighty power.'
Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John up onto a high mountain by themselves, and then his body was changed before them.
His royal garments became exceedingly sparkling-white, whiter than any launderer in the world could make them so.
Then Elijah together with Moses appeared to them, and the two were conversing with Jesus.
Peter spoke to Jesus, saying, 'Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let us build three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.'
(He did not know what to say, for they were exceedingly terrified.)
Then a cloud came and covered them, and a voice came out of that cloud saying, 'This is my beloved Son; listen to him.'
Suddenly when they looked around, they saw no one except Jesus who was with them.
As they were coming down from the mountain, he commanded them not to tell anyone what they had seen, until the Son of Man should rise from the dead.
They kept this matter to themselves, but they discussed among themselves, 'What does "rise from the dead" mean?'
They then humbly asked him, 'Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?'

He said to them, 'Elijah does come first, and he will restore all things to their former state. But why is it written concerning the Son of Man that he must suffer many things and be despised and insulted?
I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, just as it is written concerning him.'
When he returned to the disciples, he saw a large crowd surrounding them, and scribes were arguing with them.
As soon as all the crowd saw him, they were utterly astounded, and ran to greet him.
He asked them, 'What are you all arguing about with them?'
Someone in the crowd replied to him, 'Teacher, I brought my son to you; he is possessed by a mute spirit.
Whenever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and his body becomes rigid. I asked your disciples to cast it out, but they could not.'
He answered them, 'O faithless generation! How long must I be with you? How long must I bear with you? Bring him to me.'
They brought the child to him. When that spirit saw him, immediately it shook the child in a violent convulsion, and the child fell on the ground, rolling back and forth, foaming at the mouth.
He asked the child's father, 'How long has he been like this?' He replied, 'From childhood.
Many times it has tried to throw him into fire or into water to kill him. But if you can do anything, please have compassion and help us.'
Jesus said to him, '"If you can" — is that so? All things are possible for the one who believes.'
Immediately the child's father cried out, 'I believe; please help me in my unbelief.'
When Jesus saw that a crowd was running in and surrounding them, he rebuked that unclean spirit, saying to it, 'You mute and deaf demon, I command you: come out of him and do not enter him again.'
The spirit cried out loudly and shook the child violently, then it came out. The child became like a dead person, so that many said, 'He has died.'
But Jesus took the child's hand and helped lift him up, and he stood up.
When he had entered the house, his disciples privately asked him, 'Why could we not cast it out?'
He said to them, 'This kind of demon cannot be cast out by any other means, except by prayer alone.'
They left that place and traveled through the region of Galilee; he did not want anyone to know.

For he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, 'The Son of Man is about to be handed over into the hands of men; they will execute him, and after he has been executed, he will rise again on the third day.'
But they did not understand that utterance, and they feared to ask him.
They came to the city of Capernaum. When he was dwelling in the house, he asked the disciples, 'What were you arguing about among yourselves along the way?'
They were silent and did not answer, for along the way they had argued about who would be greatest among them.
He sat down, called the twelve disciples, and said to them, 'If anyone wants to be great and first, that person must be the last of all and the servant of all.'
Then he brought a little child to stand in the midst of them, and embraced the child in his royal arms, then said to them,
'Whoever receives one such little child in my name, that one receives me; and whoever receives me, receives not only me but also the royal one who sent me.'
John said to him, 'Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your royal name, so we forbade him because he did not follow with us.'
But Jesus said, 'Do not forbid him at all, for there is no one who performs mighty works in my name who can turn and revile me in an instant.
For whoever is not against us, that one is on our side.
I tell you the truth: whoever gives you a cup of water to drink, because you belong to the Christ, that person will certainly not lose his reward.
Whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble astray — if a great millstone were tied to his neck and he were thrown down into the sea, it would be better for him.

If your hand causes you to stumble astray, cut it off and throw it away. To enter into life with a stump-hand is better than having two hands and having to go into hell, into the fire that does not know how to be extinguished.
And if your foot causes you to stumble astray, cut it off and throw it away. To enter into life with a lame leg is better than having two feet and being thrown down into hell.
And if your eye causes you to stumble astray, gouge it out. To enter into the kingdom of God with one eye is better than having two eyes and being thrown down into hell,
Where 'their worm does not know how to die, and the fire does not know how to be extinguished.'
For every person will be coated with fire.
Salt is a good thing, but if salt loses its saltiness, with what will you make it taste salty again? Have salt within yourselves, and dwell in peace toward one another.
